input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Need to configure BenchmarkDotNet for multi-runtime comparison.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet handles multi-targeting automatically when you run from one framework. Let me run from net10.0 (the newest SDK) and configure the benchmark to run against both runtimes.

Actually, BenchmarkDotNet's `SimpleJob` won't automatically run both runtimes. I need to use `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net90)]` and `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net100)]` attributes to run on both. Let me update the benchmark.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-854c3fe059d54f27b6b02ac71fda6c43/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net10.0 -- --filter '*' 2>&1

data.content: BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't have a .NET 10 moniker. I'll run separate benchmarks on each framework and compare.
data.reasoningText:
So BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 only supports up to `Net90`. It doesn't have a `Net100` moniker. I need a different approach. I can either:

1. Use a custom job configuration with a custom runtime
2. Simply run two separate benchmark runs - one on net9.0 and one on net10.0 - and compare manually
3. Try a newer/preview version of BenchmarkDotNet

Let me go with option 2 - run on each framework separately. This is simpler and will get us results quickly.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-854c3fe059d54f27b6b02ac71fda6c43/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net9.0 -- --filter '*' 2>&1
